issue.body:
### Goal
The review a developer READS names which lens flagged each finding and stops overclaiming on clean reviews — putting the North Star's honesty on the surface people actually see (not just eval/RESULTS.md). Council-driven (five-lens Designer + Realistic-User).

### Context / background
Traces to NORTH_STAR ("posts like a good colleague... refuses a single flattering blended number") and M6 (feedback needs per-persona attribution AT RENDER time). Provenance already exists: `Finding.source`/`rule` and `AggregatedFinding.sources` (`src/output/aggregate.ts`) carry which persona(s) produced a finding, but `renderSticky`/`renderInlineBody` (`src/output/render.ts`) never render it. `(×N)` consensus doesn't say WHICH lenses agreed. The clean-review line "No blocking issues found. ✅" implies verified-clean but means "the enabled lenses found nothing."

### Acceptance criteria (ATDD — each is a checkable test)
- `renderSticky`: each finding line shows its lens label(s) from `AggregatedFinding.sources`. Test: a finding with `sources:["warden"]` renders a "Security" (or `warden`) tag on its line.
- Consensus names the agreeing lenses: `×2 (Correctness, Security)` not bare `(×2)`. Test on a 2-source aggregated finding.
- Clean review no longer prints a bare overclaiming checkmark. Test asserts the message states it reflects the ENABLED lenses and is "no obvious issues found," not a guarantee (assert the honest phrasing present, the bare "No blocking issues found. ✅" absent).
- A one-line **honesty footer** on the sticky: reviewed-by roster (the lens labels that ran) + the model/lane + "findings reflect enabled lenses only." Test asserts roster + "enabled lenses" present.
- Inline comment bodies carry the lens label (via `renderInlineBody`). Test.
- Persona labels: add an optional `label` to the persona schema (defaults to `id`); friendly labels in `DEFAULT_PERSONAS` (sentinel→Correctness, warden→Security, chromatic→CSS, foreman→Build/Config, stevedore→Docker, marshal→CI). Test: label falls back to id when unset; round-trips through config.

### Evidence / checks
`bun run verify:pr` green; the render/inline/config tests above. Optional live dogfood: the next self-review sticky shows lens labels + footer + honest clean phrasing.

### Non-goals
No NUMERIC recall claim yet (needs the hardened eval — a follow-up). No config for enabling/disabling the footer. No doctor routing-preview (separate issue).

### Stop condition
The clean-review wording is product copy — pick honest phrasing; surface if a specific claim ("no obvious issues") risks overpromising and you want maintainer wording.
issue.title: Honesty in the review output: persona attribution + no-overclaim on clean

pr.body:
Closes #37. **Council-decided next step** (both the custom + five-lens councils flagged the review OUTPUT as the North Star's most-visible honesty surface).

## What changed — honesty on the surface developers read
- **Attribution**: each sticky finding names the lens that flagged it (friendly label); consensus shows *which* lenses agreed (`×2: Correctness, Security`) not a bare `×2`. Inline comments carry the lens label too.
- **No overclaim on clean**: instead of "No blocking issues found ✅", the clean line names the enabled lenses + model and says it means "nothing obvious was found by the enabled lenses, **not that the change is verified correct**." (Directly fixes the "flattering number" failure the North Star forbids, on the most-seen surface.)
- **Honesty footer**: `Reviewed by: <lenses> · <model> · findings reflect the enabled lenses only`.
- Personas gain an optional `label` (defaults to id); `DEFAULT_PERSONAS` → Correctness/Security/CSS/Build-Config/Docker/CI.

Attribution is at **pass granularity** — a batched pass's findings are honestly labelled with all its lenses (the structurer can't sub-attribute within a merged prompt); that's a real, honest limitation, not a bug.

## Why this first
Both councils converged: distribution is premature, M6 is blocked, and the review output "anonymizes its own reviewers" + "quietly overclaims on clean" — the exact honesty gap the North Star exists to close. It's also M6's precondition (feedback needs per-lens attribution at render time). Low-risk: the provenance data already existed in `AggregatedFinding.sources`.

## Risk summary
Pure rendering/attribution change; no trust-boundary/Poster/gather files touched. Regenerated the repo's own `.squarewright.yml` (personas now carry labels — drift test guards it).

## Reviewer result
`sqw-reviewer` gate pending. Note: this PR should also trigger a **live self-review** — watch whether its own sticky now shows lens labels + the honesty footer + the honest clean phrasing.

## Commands / CI
`bun run verify:pr` green (72 tests, +5: inline label, attribution, consensus-names, honest-clean-with-footer, single-lens tag).

## What I need from the maintainer
Product-copy check on the clean-review wording ("nothing obvious was found… not that the change is verified correct") and the footer — flag if you'd word the honesty differently. A NUMERIC recall caveat (e.g. "catches ~1 in N historically") is deferred until the eval is hardened (#38).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FTat69PTDspaT5mNGt76vX
pr.title: feat(output): persona attribution + honesty footer in the review (#37)
